heading: Procurement of services: tracking of purchases
label: 4505 Procurement of services: tracking of purchases
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (e)(3), was repealed by , , 135 Stat 1825.Section 235 of this titlePub. L. 117–81, div. A, title VIII, § 815(b)(1)Dec. 27, 2021

A, title VIII, § 807(b)122 Stat. 215Jan. 28, 2008“(1)The amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall be effective upon the date of the enactment of this Act [].Jan. 28, 2008“(2)The first inventory required by , United States Code [now ], as added by subsection (a), shall be submitted not later than the end of the third quarter of fiscal year 2008.”section 2330a(c) of title 1010 U.S.C. 4505(c)

children.text: Logistics management services.
Equipment related services.
Knowledge-based services.
Electronics and communications services.
The services purchased.
The total dollar amount of the purchase.
The form of contracting action used to make the purchase.

children.label: A
children.text: a performance-based contract, performance-based task order, or other performance-based arrangement that contains firm fixed prices for the specific tasks to be performed;

children.label: B
children.text: any other performance-based contract, performance-based task order, or performance-based arrangement; or

children.label: C
children.text: any contract, task order, or other arrangement that is not performance based.
In the case of a purchase made through an agency other than the Department of Defense, the agency through which the purchase is made.
The extent of competition provided in making the purchase and whether there was more than one offer.

children.label: A
children.text: a small business concern;

children.label: B
children.text: a small business concern owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals; or

children.label: C
children.text: a small business concern owned and controlled by women.

children.label: i
children.text: the collection of data regarding functions and missions performed by contractors in a manner that is comparable to the manpower data elements used in inventories of functions performed by Department of Defense employees;

children.label: ii
children.text: the calculation of contractor full-time equivalents for direct labor, using direct labor hours in a manner that is comparable to the calculation of Department of Defense civilian full-time employees; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the conduct and completion of the annual review required under subsection (e)(1).

children.label: B
children.text: The Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment shall be responsible for developing guidance on other data elements and implementing procedures for requirements relating to acquisition.

children.label: A
children.text: The functions and missions performed by the contractor.

children.label: B
children.text: The contracting organization, the component of the Department of Defense administering the contract, and the organization whose requirements are being met through contractor performance of the function.

children.label: C
children.text: The funding source for the contract under which the function is performed by appropriation and operating agency.

children.label: D
children.text: The fiscal year for which the activity first appeared on an inventory under this section.

children.label: E
children.text: The number of contractor employees, expressed as full-time equivalents for direct labor, using direct labor hours and associated cost data collected from contractors (except that estimates may be used where such data is not available and cannot reasonably be made available in a timely manner for the purpose of the inventory).

children.label: F
children.text: A determination whether the contract pursuant to which the activity is performed is a personal services contract.

children.label: G
children.text: A summary of the data required to be collected for the activity under subsection (a).
The inventory required under this subsection shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.

children.label: A
children.text: Special studies or analysis that is not research and development.

children.label: B
children.text: Information technology and telecommunications.

children.label: C
children.text: Support, including professional, administrative, and management;

children.label: A
children.text: each contract on the list that is a personal services contract has been entered into, and is being performed, in accordance with applicable statutory and regulatory requirements;

children.label: B
children.text: the activities on the list do not include any inherently governmental functions; and

children.label: C
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, the activities on the list do not include any functions closely associated with inherently governmental functions; and

children.label: A
children.text: to performance by civilian employees of the Department of Defense pursuant to ; orsection 2463 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: to an acquisition approach that would be more advantageous to the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Development of Plan and Enforcement and Approval Mechanisms
children.label: e .—Development of Plan and Enforcement and Approval Mechanisms

children.text: provide for the use of the inventory by the military department or Defense Agency to implement the requirements of ;section 129a of this title
ensure the inventory is used to inform strategic workforce planning;
facilitate use of the inventory for compliance with section 235 of this title; and1See References in Text note below.1
provide for appropriate consideration of the conversion of activities identified under subsection (e)(3) within a reasonable period of time.

children.heading: .—Comptroller General Report
children.label: f .—Comptroller General Report
children.text: Not later than , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the congressional defense committees a report on the status of the data collection required in subsection (a) and an assessment of the efforts by the Department of Defense to implement subsection (e).March 31, 2018

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: g .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize the performance of personal services by a contractor except where expressly authorized by a provision of law other than this section.

children.heading: .—Performance-based
children.label: 1 .—Performance-based
children.text: The term “performance-based”, with respect to a contract, task order, or arrangement, means that the contract, task order, or arrangement, respectively, includes the use of performance work statements that set forth contract requirements in clear, specific, and objective terms with measurable outcomes.

children.heading: .—Function closely associated with inherently governmental functions
children.label: 2 .—Function closely associated with inherently governmental functions
children.text: The term “function closely associated with inherently governmental functions” has the meaning given that term in .section 4508(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: .—Inherently governmental functions
children.label: 3 .—Inherently governmental functions
children.text: The term “inherently governmental functions” has the meaning given that term in .section 4508(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Personal services contract
children.label: 4 .—Personal services contract
children.text: The term “personal services contract” means a contract under which, as a result of its terms or conditions or the manner of its administration during performance, contractor personnel are subject to the relatively continuous supervision and control of one or more Government officers or employees, except that the giving of an order for a specific article or service, with the right to reject the finished product or result, is not the type of supervision or control that makes a contract a personal services contract.

children.heading: .—Service acquisition portfolio groups
children.label: 5 .—Service acquisition portfolio groups
children.text: The term “service acquisition portfolio groups” means the groups identified in Department of Defense Instruction 5000.74, Defense Acquisition of Services () or successor guidance.January 5, 2016

children.heading: .—Staff augmentation contracts
children.label: 6 .—Staff augmentation contracts
children.text: The term “staff augmentation contracts” means services contracts for personnel who are physically present in a Government work space on a full-time or permanent part-time basis, for the purpose of advising on, providing support to, or assisting a Government agency in the performance of the agency’s missions, including authorized personal services contracts.

children.heading: .—Simplified acquisition threshold
children.label: 7 .—Simplified acquisition threshold
children.text: The term “simplified acquisition threshold” has the meaning given the term in .section 134 of title 41

children.label: A
children.text: The term “small business concern” has the meaning given such term under section 3 of the Small Business Act ().15 U.S.C. 632

children.label: B
children.text: The terms “small business concern owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals” and “small business concern owned and controlled by women” have the meanings given such terms, respectively, in section 8(d)(3) of the Small Business Act ().15 U.S.C. 637(d)(3)
